user_query:
I'm compiling an internal ops newsletter and need fast, structured extractive summaries from two articles we've exported as HTML. Please read both files under input/:
- input/supply_chain.html
- input/capability_primer.html

For each article:
1) Extract clean text from the HTML (ignore navigation, headers/footers, scripts, styles) and perform extractive summarization selecting 15–20 key sentences that best represent the content.
2) Produce a structured markdown summary with the following sections and fields in this exact order and with these exact headings:
   - "# Content Summary"
   - "**Source:**" followed by the input file path
   - "**Title:**" using the HTML <title> value
   - "**Stats:** ~X words | ~Y min read" (X and Y must be integers; compute Y using 200 words per minute, rounded down)
   - "## Key Sentences (Extractive Summary)" with 15–20 non-empty lines, one sentence per line
   - "## Structured Summary Template" with filled prose (no TODOs) for: What is this about? Main arguments or findings, Why it matters
   - "## Tweet Hook" with a single line starting with "> " and no more than 280 characters
   - "## Key Takeaways" as a numbered list with at least 3 items (lines starting with "1.", "2.", "3." etc.)
   - "## AI Enhancement Prompt" that embeds the extracted article text in a ready-to-use prompt. Include a clear label like "Article text:" and paste the cleaned text after it (no placeholders).

3) Save each file to output/summaries/{basename}.md, where {basename} matches the input filename without extension (e.g., output/summaries/supply_chain.md and output/summaries/capability_primer.md).

Additionally, create a combined index file at output/index.json containing an array of two objects summarizing each article with the following fields: source_filename (string, the input path), title (string), word_count (integer from the Stats line), reading_time_minutes (integer from the Stats line), key_sentence_count (integer, number of lines in the Key Sentences section), and tweet_hook_length (integer, character count of the tweet hook line without the leading "> ").

Constraints and notes:
- Use only the provided HTML files; do not fetch external URLs.
- All sections must be fully written with meaningful content; do not leave placeholders or TODO markers.
- Ensure the tweet hook is no longer than 280 characters.
- The AI Enhancement Prompt must include the cleaned article text after a label like "Article text:".
- Output paths must be exactly as specified under output/.
